message.content:
{
  "document_metadata": {
    "board": "CBSE",
    "subject": "Mathematics",
    "class": "IX",
    "grade": "9",
    "stream": "",
    "academic_year": "2026-27",
    "total_pages": "13"
  },
  "classes": [
    {
      "class_name": "Class IX",
      "subject": "Mathematics",
      "part_or_section": "Curriculum Structure and Question Paper Design",
      "units": [
        {
          "unit_id": "U1",
          "unit_name": "Number System",
          "marks": 7,
          "headings": [
            "Introduction to rational numbers",
            "Representation of rational numbers on the number line",
            "Density of rational numbers and its proof",
            "Finding rational numbers between any two rational numbers",
            "Decimal representation of rational numbers",
            "Introduction to irrational numbers",
            "Proof of irrationality of √2 and √3",
            "The square root spiral"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Number System"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Rational Numbers",
            "Irrational Numbers",
            "Density of Rational Numbers",
            "Decimal Representation",
            "Square Root Spiral"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Properties of rational numbers",
            "Proofs of irrationality",
            "Computational thinking for number representation"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U2",
          "unit_name": "Algebra",
          "marks": 20,
          "headings": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials",
            "Sequences and Progressions",
            "Exploring Algebraic Identities",
            "Linear Equations in Two Variables"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials",
            "Sequences and Progressions",
            "Exploring Algebraic Identities",
            "Linear Equations in Two Variables"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Polynomials",
            "Algebraic Expressions",
            "Degree of a Polynomial",
            "Linear Relationships",
            "Sequences and Progressions (AP, GP)",
            "Fractals",
            "Tower of Hanoi Puzzle",
            "Algebraic Identities",
            "Factorisation",
            "Rational Expressions",
            "Linear Equations in Two Variables"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Slope and y-intercept",
            "Recursive and Explicit rules",
            "Visualising identities using geometrical models",
            "Graphical method for solving pairs of linear equations",
            "Substitution and elimination methods",
            "Consistency and inconsistency"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U3",
          "unit_name": "Coordinate Geometry",
          "marks": 4,
          "headings": [
            "Brief history of coordinate geometry",
            "The 2-D Cartesian coordinate system",
            "Distance between two points in the 2-D plane",
            "Midpoint of the line-segment between two points"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Coordinate Geometry"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Cartesian Coordinate System",
            "Distance Formula",
            "Midpoint Formula",
            "Collinearity Check",
            "Right-angled Triangle Verification"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Representing floor plans on a grid",
            "Computational thinking for geometric properties"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U4",
          "unit_name": "Geometry",
          "marks": 25,
          "headings": [
            "Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates",
            "Lines and Angles",
            "Triangles – Congruence Theorems",
            "4-gons (Quadrilaterals)",
            "Circles"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Introduction to Euclid's Geometry",
            "Lines and Angles",
            "Triangles: Congruence Theorems",
            "Quadrilaterals",
            "Circles"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Euclidean Axioms and Postulates",
            "Baudhayana's Sulbasutras",
            "Angles (Rays, Measures, Pairs)",
            "Parallelism Theorems",
            "Triangle Congruence Conditions (SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, AAS)",
            "Propositions and Converse",
            "Quadrilateral Properties and Midpoint Theorem",
            "Circle Definitions and Chords",
            "Cyclic Quadrilaterals"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Proof by contradiction",
            "Triangle rigidity",
            "Symmetries of 4-gons (Reflection, Rotation)",
            "Tiling a plane with 4-gons",
            "Angle subtended by an arc at the centre and circumference"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U5",
          "unit_name": "Mensuration",
          "marks": 14,
          "headings": [
            "Area and Perimeter",
            "Surface Area and Volume"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Perimeter of shapes",
            "Heron's formula",
            "Brahmagupta's formula",
            "Squaring a rectangle",
            "Circumference and Arc length",
            "Surface Area and Volume (Cube, Cuboid, Cylinder, Cone, Pyramid, Sphere)"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Perimeter of shapes",
            "Area of rectangles, parallelograms, triangles",
            "Heron's formula",
            "Brahmagupta's formula for cyclic quadrilaterals",
            "Surface Area and Volume formulas",
            "Historical approximations to Pi"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Special cases vs Generalisation in geometry",
            "Squaring a rectangle (Proof from Sulbasutras)",
            "Area of sector and segment of circle",
            "Computational thinking for 3-D shapes"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U6",
          "unit_name": "Statistics and Probability",
          "marks": 10,
          "headings": [
            "Graphical representation of data",
            "Measures of central tendency",
            "Concept of probability and randomness",
            "Empirical vs Theoretical probability"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Statistics",
            "Introduction to Probability"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Data Collection and Visualisation",
            "Weighted Average, Mean, Median, Mode",
            "Stacked Bar Graphs (100%)",
            "Probability Scale",
            "Empirical Probability",
            "Theoretical Probability"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Tree diagrams and tables for probability",
            "Sample space and events",
            "Pattern recognition in random experiments"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "formative_only_content": [],
  "practicals": [
    {
      "title": "Lab Practical (Mathematics Laboratory)",
      "source_text": "Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books. Prescribed Books: Mathematics - Textbook for class IX, Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX, Laboratory Manual - Mathematics, secondary stage.",
      "related_unit": "All Units",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "activities": [
    {
      "title": "Square Root Spiral Construction",
      "source_text": "Construct the square root spiral. Apply computational thinking to represent rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models.",
      "related_unit": "Number System",
      "related_chapter": "Number System"
    },
    {
      "title": "Tower of Hanoi Puzzle",
      "source_text": "Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle. Use computational thinking to identify patterns, write step-by-step rules.",
      "related_unit": "Algebra",
      "related_chapter": "Sequences and Progressions"
    },
    {
      "title": "Geometric Models for Identities",
      "source_text": "Visualise algebraic identities using geometric models. Visualising factorisation of quadratic expressions through algebra tiles.",
      "related_unit": "Algebra",
      "related_chapter": "Exploring Algebraic Identities"
    },
    {
      "title": "Floor Plan Representation",
      "source_text": "Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates. Compute the distance between two points.",
      "related_unit": "Coordinate Geometry",
      "related_chapter": "Coordinate Geometry"
    },
    {
      "title": "Euclid's Definitions and Constructions",
      "source_text": "Constructing a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras. Justify simple constructions using axioms.",
      "related_unit": "Geometry",
      "related_chapter": "Introduction to Euclid's Geometry"
    },
    {
      "title": "Paper Manipulation Activities",
      "source_text": "Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.",
      "related_unit": "Geometry",
      "related_chapter": "Quadrilaterals"
    },
    {
      "title": "Cultural Motifs Analysis",
      "source_text": "Identify cultural motifs involving circles, for example, the Dharmachakra, Ashoka Chakra, Sudarshan Chakra.",
      "related_unit": "Geometry",
      "related_chapter": "Circles"
    },
    {
      "title": "Squaring a Rectangle Proof",
      "source_text": "Explain the classical problem of 'squaring' a given shape. Explain how ancient civilisations approximated the area of a circle.",
      "related_unit": "Mensuration",
      "related_chapter": "Area and Perimeter"
    },
    {
      "title": "Data Analysis Projects",
      "source_text": "Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data to answer a statistical investigative question. Apply computational thinking strategies to analyse real-life data.",
      "related_unit": "Statistics and Probability",
      "related_chapter": "Statistics"
    },
    {
      "title": "Random Experiment Simulation",
      "source_text": "Use computational thinking strategies, such as pattern recognition and simulation, to model random experiments and estimate probabilities.",
      "related_unit": "Statistics and Probability",
      "related_chapter": "Introduction to Probability"
    }
  ],
  "projects": [
    {
      "title": "Portfolio Assessment",
      "source_text": "Internal Assessment: Portfolio (05 Marks). Continuous and holistic assessment through projects, activities, and investigations forms an integral part of the learning process.",
      "related_unit": "All Units",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Mathematical Modelling Projects",
      "source_text": "Models daily-life phenomena and uses representations (graphs, tables, equations) to draw conclusions. Models real-life situations in the form of equations.",
      "related_unit": "Algebra / Statistics",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "assessment_information": {
    "marks_distribution": [
      "Total Marks: 80 (Theory) + 20 (Internal Assessment)",
      "Unit I (Number System): 7 Marks",
      "Unit II (Algebra): 20 Marks",
      "Unit III (Coordinate Geometry): 4 Marks",
      "Unit IV (Geometry): 25 Marks",
      "Unit V (Mensuration): 14 Marks",
      "Unit VI (Statistics and Probability): 10 Marks",
      "Internal Assessment: 20 Marks",
      "- Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment: 10 Marks",
      "- Portfolio: 5 Marks",
      "- Lab Practical: 5 Marks"
    ],
    "excluded_or_non_assessed_content": [],
    "teacher_notes": [
      "Curriculum redesigned in alignment with NEP 2020 and NCF-SE 2023.",
      "Prioritize deep conceptual understanding, logical reasoning, and competency-based education over rote learning.",
      "Integrate Indian Knowledge System (e.g., Baudhayana's Sulbasutras, Brahmagupta, Aryabhata) with contemporary knowledge.",
      "Focus on problem-solving, visualisation, mathematical modelling, communication, computational thinking, and data analytics.",
      "Emphasis on real-life applications and experiential learning.",
      "Continuous assessment through projects, activities, and investigations is integral; move beyond summative examinations only.",
      "Prescribed Books: NCERT Textbook for Class IX, CBSE Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools (Class IX), NCERT Laboratory Manual."
    ]
  }
}
